creator: alk@pobox.com
text:
Using current ppp with aliasing, rcmds fail.

Nota Bene:
  If I 'cd usr.sbin/ppp;cvs update -D 'June 21' .;make install', then
  the problem goes away.

Fix: 

whoops, time's up.
How-To-Repeat: 
; cat /etc/ppp/ppp.conf
default:
 allow users alk
 load ptech

basic:
 allow users alk
 accept chap
 accept pap
 disable chap
 disable pap
 alias enable yes
 set mtu 1500
 set server /tmp/ppp ********
 set log +connect +phase 

ptech:
 allow users alk
 load basic
 set authname alk
 set authkey ********
 set phone 7397050
 set login "TIMEOUT 15 Session"
 set dial "ABORT BUSY ABORT NO\\sCARRIER TIMEOUT 4 \"\" ATY0 OK ATDT\\T TIMEOUT 45 CONNECT"
 set device /dev/cuaa0
 set redial 5 2
 set speed 115200
 set timeout 0

ptech2:
 allow users alk
 load ptech
 set phone 7367388

dial:
 allow users alk
 load ptech
 dial

#
# pilot servers
#

pnet:
 allow users alk
 set timeout 0
 enable proxy
 set device localhost:6999
 set ifaddr 172.24.0.1 192.168.0.7

pcom:
 allow users alk
 set timeout 0
 enable proxy
 set speed 9600
 set device /dev/cuaa1
 set ifaddr 172.16.0.1 192.168.0.7
; ppp
Working in interactive mode
Using interface: tun0
ppp ON compound> dial

[then try:]

; rsh somehost.org date
select: protocol failure in circuit setup

[i.e /usr/src/lib/libc/net/rcmd.c:176]
